mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 payroll level 134,041k suggests ongoing post-recession hiring
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS 3-month growth rate versus 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months produce negative forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising month-over-month, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This contractionary monetary regime compresses equity multiples and raises discount rates, producing negative 20-day forward returns as market participants anticipate slower growth and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 FEDFUNDS at 3.62% and CPIAUCSL at 198.5 indicate tightening bias
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: When trigger condition is met, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term versus forward returns is < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations. This demand-side contraction produces negative 20-day forward returns as equity investors reprice earnings downward and reduce growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 RSAFS at 351,461M reflects post-crisis demand normalization
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following trigger activation are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC of RSAFS growth versus 60-day returns is < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month trend, indicating strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This supply expansion supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive 5-day forward returns as market participants recognize improved operational efficiency and output growth.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 INDPRO at 118.016 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month growth versus 5-day SPX returns is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth produces negative 5-day returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously, indicating synchronized demand destruction across residential and consumer sectors. This joint demand shock signals recession risk and produces negative 20-day forward returns as equity investors reprice systematic growth risk downward.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 HOUST at 2,009k and RSAFS at 351,461M show stabilization post-crisis
failure_condition: When both demand proxies decline simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC is < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) remains below 100,000 persons monthly, signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk. This dual labor-market weakness produces negative 60-day forward returns as equity investors anticipate earnings compression from reduced consumer spending and corporate cost pressures.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 UNRATE at 5.1% and PAYEMS at 134,041k show labor market recovery
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession regime
failure_condition: When both labor conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC is < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation rate) turn negative, indicating monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and supports asset valuations. This negative real rate regime produces positive 20-day forward returns as investors rotate into equities seeking real return preservation.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2011 FEDFUNDS at 3.62% with CPIAUCSL at 198.5 implies positive real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation or negative real rate regime
failure_condition: When real rates turn negative, 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC is < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,500k units, indicating synchronized demand weakness across macro and housing sectors. This dual demand contraction produces negative 60-day forward returns as equity investors anticipate earnings downgrades and recession entry.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2% annualized AND HOUST < 1,500k
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2010 GDPC1 at 11,089.2B and April 2011 HOUST at 2,009k show post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: When both demand indicators weaken simultaneously, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC is < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
